mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 during rate decline periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting higher corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive quarterly returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which reduces input cost pressures and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth coincides with negative 20-day equity returns in > 70% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive inflation surprises yield positive 5-day equity returns in > 60% of cases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightness, supporting wage growth and consumer spending that drive positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive quarterly returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 55% over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods show negative mean 20-day returns or Rank IC < 0.015 over a 3-year rolling window, indicating decoupling between macro output and equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and weakening manufacturing demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions of this magnitude are followed by positive 5-day equity returns in > 65% of instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.
